grammar: question word order (What is the weather like?)
question_types: narrative passage (~120-150 words) with 4-6 linked comprehension questions (who/where/why/how-felt)
dialogue fill-in-the-blank across varied settings (reception desk, dinner table, canteen)
cloze passage with numbered blanks (clothing/vocabulary theme)
reading_patterns: narrative recount of a school outing or exhibition visit, told in past tense, followed by questions on character relationships, location, motivation, and emotional reaction
short factual/procedural text (e.g. traffic light rule) tested via single inference question
dialogue_patterns: problem-sharing exchange (failed test, awkward mistake) -> sympathetic/advice reply
family exchange (child asking parent) -> reply completes a request or permission pattern
distractor_patterns: grammatically similar but word-order-incorrect question form (e.g. 'What is like the weather?')
emotionally mismatched reply (positive reply to a negative situation or vice versa)
correct vocabulary but wrong register (too formal/informal for context)
detail from a different part of the narrative attributed to the wrong character
answer_logic: narrative comprehension requires linking a character's stated action to their later stated feeling
functional-language items require matching emotional tone (sympathy vs. celebration) to the described situation
word-order/question-form items test recognition of the single grammatically correct variant among near-miss distractors
notes: Extracted from native embedded PDF text via pdftotext. Longer/denser narrative passage than 2558-2559; adds punctuation and intonation-pattern item types.
